reasoning: Phase B Task B6 ships an inline critic-feedback surface for the IA + Interview wizard steps. New <CriticFeedbackPanel> renders a @caia/ui Accordion of modification items with per-item severity + category Badges and per-item checkboxes; the primary Apply&rerun button POSTs the selected modification ids back to the supplied rerunEndpoint, a ghost Dismiss button hides the panel via the parent-owned onDismiss callback, and the panel surfaces a loading state mid-flight + an inline error message on non-2xx responses. Two thin client bridges (ArchitectureCriticBridge / InterviewCriticBridge) own the dismissal state and feed the panel a step-specific feedback envelope; they exist because the panel is 'use client' and the wizard pages are server components — the bridges are the client boundary that owns the panel's lifecycle props. V1 wiring uses compile-time stub modifications keyed by a searchParam (criticKind=approved-with-modifications | coverage-insufficient) so the panel UX is reviewable without a live runIA / live interview-critic call. Wave 2 swaps the stub for the Pg-backed verdict shape. 16 vitest cases (brief asked for >=15). Whole wizard suite green at 326/326. Reuse-first: every visible primitive comes from @caia/ui (Accordion, AccordionItem, AccordionTrigger, AccordionContent, Card, CardHeader, CardTitle, CardDescription, CardContent, Badge, Button). No raw shadcn/Radix imports. No third-party accordion. fetchImpl is the test seam; production uses the global fetch.

reuseSearchResults.reason: The @caia/ui accordion is a pure-Tailwind primitive ready to swap for the Radix-backed one when @radix-ui/react-accordion lands. Public API is stable. Mandatory per ADR-065 / caia-no-raw-radix-outside-ui-package.

Standard primitives. The destructive variant on Badge matches the p0/p1 severity surface; the outline variant matches p3 / category tags. Selecting any non-@caia/ui Badge or Card would fail the reuse-advisory-blocking gate.
The brief explicitly asks for an inline accordion surface, not a modal. A Dialog would also force the user to make a binary choice (apply all / cancel) instead of per-item selection.

A Set<string> + useState is enough for 2–10 checkbox items. Adding a form library would be a new dependency for a UI surface that has no validation, no submission, and no async-validation requirement.

The package is the upstream source of the verdict, but the panel does not invoke the critic — it consumes the verdict shape the wizard page hands it. Wave 2 wires the panel's `feedback` prop directly off the runIA / runInterviewCritic response; B6 keeps the panel agnostic so it can render against the stub envelope today.

reuseSearchResults.candidate: B7 wizardWithRetry / B3 withClaudeSpawnerSpan for the rerun POST
reuseSearchResults.reason: The panel POSTs to `feedback.rerunEndpoint`, which IS one of the existing wizard route handlers — those routes already use the canonical retry + span chain. The panel does not duplicate that wrapper on the client; it just hands the route the body.

modifications: criticKind is sourced from a searchParam in V1 wiring rather than from a Pg-backed read of the most recent runIA verdict. This is documented inline as a Wave-2 follow-up so the operator can preview the panel UX without a live critic invocation.

The interview rerun endpoint is `/api/wizard/interview/complete` because the existing route already returns 412 + coverage diagnostics when the critic verdict is `coverage-insufficient`. The architecture rerun endpoint is `/api/wizard/architecture/run` — which does not yet exist on develop. Wave 2 ships that route; until then, the architecture panel renders + the rerun POST will 404. Documented inline.

Two bridges (ArchitectureCriticBridge / InterviewCriticBridge) rather than one polymorphic component. The duplication is tiny (~50 lines each) and keeps the per-step stub modifications + rerun endpoint co-located with the bridge — easier to evolve as Wave 2 lands each step's live verdict.

evidence.typecheckDelta: no new TS errors in B6 files
